Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Deposition And For Protective Order To Preclude Deposition Of Attorney Susan T. Kumagai
Off Calendar Jul-08-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. STEVEN L. DERBY, Esq. of Derby, McGuinness & Goldsmith, LLP (Tel: 510-987-8778) for plaintiff Todd J. Ferraris;
Atty. CARA F. BARRICK, Esq. of Ogletree, Deakins, Nash, Smoak & Stewart, P.C. (Tel: 415-442-4810) for defendant United Parcel Service, Inc.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT UNITED PARCEL SERVICE, INC.'s Motion To Stay Deposition And For Protective Order To Preclude Deposition Of Attorney Susan T. Kumagai.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem. The Judge Pro Tem and and counsel for both sides (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's motion for protective order is granted. Depositions of opposing counsel are presumptively improper, severely restricted, and require "extremely" good cause. A three-prong test is used to consider propriety of attorney depositions. Does the proponent have other practicable means to obtain the information? Is the information crucial to the preparation of the case? Is the information subject to a privilege? See Spectra-Physics, Inc. v. Superior Court (1988) 198 Cal.App.3d 1487. The party seeking an attorney deposition has the burden to establish predicate facts to meet the first two prongs. Plaintiff has failed to show he lacks other practicable means of obtaining the information sought and the information is crucial to his case. The attorney's letter to the DFEH investigator would result in deposition questioning which would directly and indirectly lead to exploration of matters protected by the attorney-client and attorney work-product privileges. See: Carehouse Convalescent Hospital, 143 Cal.App.4th 1558 (2006).

Defense counsel is to prepare and submit a proposed order to opposing counsel for review. The parties are then to submit either an approved proposed order or competing proposed orders in Word format to the Judge Pro Tem at sbs@sbslawsf.com.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF TODD FERRARIS' Motion For Progressive Sanctions For Willful Disobediance Of Court Order.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem. The Judge Pro Tem and counsel for parties all stipualte to the clerk signing the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

The Court's tentative ruling instructed the parties to appear.

Matter argued.

The Court continues the matter for further hearing to December 29, 2020 at 9:00 a.m. on the Discovery Calendar.

The Court orders the plaintiff to take all best efforts to get a subpoena issued and served on Mr. Wong and to conduct the depositon of Mr. Wong. If Mr. Wong's deposition goes forward, this matter will go off calendar. If Mr. Wong fails to appear for his deposition, the Court will consider a ruling of spoilation of evidence and that it be deemed that had Mr. Wong testified, Mr. Wong's testimony would have been consistent with plaintiff's understanding.

The Court sets the following briefing schedule regarding the further hearing on December 29, 2020. Plaintiff is to file and electronically serve no later than the close of business on December 14, 2020 a memorandum setting forth the efforts plaintiff has made and the results of those efforts. If plaintiff is seeking a further order from the Court, the memorandum should address how plaintiff would like Mr. Wong's deposition testimony to be treated. Plaintiff's memorandum should cite whatever cases support plaintiff's contention that there has been "willful and intentional violation of a court order." Defendant is to file and electronically serve its response no later than the close of business on December 23, 2020. Defendants' memorandum should include any opposition to the court's suggested relief of treating the evidence as improperly withheld.

The Court does not impose any sanctions at this time and will hold off on ruling on sanctions until the further hearing on December 29, 2020.

The Court instructs plaintiff to prepare a proposed order and to serve it on defendant for comment. Plaintiff is to then email the proposed order and any comments to the Judge Pro Tem at rennepar85@gmail.com.
